'''Shuma''' was a displaced [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] businessman on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]].

The "black sheep" of the Hutts, Shuma was a legitimate businessman on the world of [[Neka]] before the [[Jedi Civil War]]. However, when the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|armada]] closed the [[Hyperlane|spacelanes]] around Neka, Shuma was forced to flee offworld through certain [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] who knew how to penetrate the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]]. Shortly after, a Sith commander gave the order to virus-bomb the planet, destroying all traces of intelligent life.

Shuma arrived on Dantooine at the [[Jedi Enclave]], where the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]] gladly gave him refuge from the war. It is unknown if he escaped death from the Sith twice when [[Darth Malak]] [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attacked Dantooine]] in [[3956 BBY]].

==Behind the scenes==
Shuma was cut from the final game of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. However, his dialogue paths still exist in the game files.
